steps.title: Identify the common standard
steps.content:
Both texts evaluate how well experimental designs exclude nutrient pathways other than CMN.

Text 1 emphasizes that Xiao used barriers to separate roots — a crucial step Wahbi did not take.

Text 2 then exposes why even barriers fail: they must be permeable to hyphae, which means they must also allow liquids.

steps.title: Apply the standard to each answer
steps.content:
Both authors would answer "No" — Wahbi's study did not effectively exclude other pathways.

Text 1 directly faults Wahbi for omitting barriers; Text 2's logic (barriers permit liquids, leaving alternative routes open) strengthens that verdict for a design without barriers.

Why other choices fail:
  • A — asks whether Xiao's barriers actually work; the authors disagree on this, so they would not both agree on an answer.
  • C — asserts a biological mechanism neither text establishes.
  • D — concerns ideal barrier materials, which only Text 2 addresses.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
